source: Historical stress scenario library for UCITS monitoring
basis: CSSF UCITS requirement for most relevant historical scenarios with holding period
notes: This is a temporary separate library from the HF scenario setup. It may later be merged into or replace the current scenario library once both UCITS and AIF historical scenario definitions are aligned.

historical_2008_black_week.description: Global Financial Crisis period capturing equity market crashes, credit spread widening, and FX volatility
historical_2008_black_week.holding_period_basis: CSSF UCITS reporting standard holding period for historical scenario calibration

interest_rates.description: Parallel rate shift (negative in financial crisis)

historical_2008_black_week.regulatory_basis: CSSF UCITS stress testing framework — most relevant historical scenario

historical_2020_covid.description: COVID-19 pandemic initial shock period with equity decline, credit widening, and FX volatility
historical_2020_covid.holding_period_basis: CSSF UCITS reporting standard holding period for historical scenario calibration

interest_rates.description: Parallel rate shift (rates cut in response)

fx.description: FX movements against EUR (USD strength, GBP weakness)

historical_2020_covid.regulatory_basis: CSSF UCITS stress testing framework — most relevant historical scenario

historical_2022_rate_inflation.description: Central bank rate hike cycle in response to inflation, with equity decline, rate increase, and spread widening
historical_2022_rate_inflation.holding_period_basis: CSSF UCITS reporting standard holding period for historical scenario calibration

equity.description: Equity market decline due to rising rates and growth concerns

interest_rates.description: Parallel rate shift (rates rising with inflation)

credit_spreads.description: Credit spread widening due to rising rates

fx.description: FX movements against EUR (USD strength from higher rates, GBP weakness)

historical_2022_rate_inflation.regulatory_basis: CSSF UCITS stress testing framework — most relevant historical scenario
